titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale University Department of Astronomy Collection of Astronomical Reference Materials
Yale University Department of Astronomy Collection of Astronomical Reference Materials
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 5, 2020 at 06:28

did.unittitle: Yale University Department of Astronomy collection of astronomical reference materials

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale University Department of Astronomy, 2010.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Star charts, undated. II. Parallaxes, 1920s-1930s. III. Other materials and photographs, 1859-1984.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale University Department of Astronomy Collection of Astronomical Reference Materials. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Astronomical reference materials, including star charts, atlases, catalogs, and maps. The collection also includes fourteen manuscript volumes documenting parallaxes, photographs of the moon taken by Lunar Orbiter I, II, and III in the 1960s, and a small amount of material related to Yale's observatory, including records of the Trustees of the Winchester Observatory from 1871.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Yale University Department of Astronomy Collection of Astronomical Reference Materials is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Star Charts in this series are arranged by their declination. Declination is comparable to geographic latitude but projected onto the celestial sphere. The arrangement starts with the southern pole star (−90°), works towards the celestial equator (0°) and ends with the northern pole star (+90°).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These folders also contain loose notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of bound manuscript astronomical parallaxes. A parallax is an apparent displacement or difference in the position of an object viewed along two different lines of sight.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in numerical order by volume number.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains four volumes: Volume 1 contains parallaxes 1 through 98. Volume 2 contains parallaxes 99 through 209. Volume 3 contains parallaxes 210 through 305, and Volume 4 contains parallaxes 305 through 399.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains four volumes: Volume 5 contains parallaxes 400 through 475. Volume 6 contains parallaxes 476 through 571. Volume 7 contains parallaxes 572 throughs 661, and Volume 8 contains parallaxes 662 through 757.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains four volumes: Volume 9 contains parallaxes 758 through 851. Volume 10 contains parallaxes 852 through 947. Volume 11 contains parallaxes 948 through 1048, and Volume 12 contains parallaxes 1049 through 1144.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This box contains two volumes: Volume 13 contains parallaxes 1145 through 1227, and Volume 14 contains parallaxes 1228 through 1323.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of material pertaining to the work of Yale's observatory, which was called the Winchester Observatory until 1920; reference materials; maps and photographs of the moon including those taken by NASA's Lunar Orbiters in the 1960s; and star charts produced by the Bonner Durchmusterung and Cordoba Durchmusterung projects in the late nineteenth through early twentieth century.

did.unittitle: Typed index to interleaved B.D. catalogues
Astronomische Beobachtungen auf der Sternwarte , by Dr. Friedrich Wilhelm August Argelander., -2° bis +19°
Astronomische Beobachtungen auf der Sternwarte , by Dr. Friedrich Wilhelm August Argelander., +41° bis +90°
Astronomische Beobachtungen auf der Sternwarte , by Dr. Friedrich Wilhelm August Argelander., -2° bis +23°
Astronomische Beobachtungen auf der Sternwarte , by Dr. Friedrich Wilhelm August Argelander.
Selected areas proper motions. Correspondence:- Dr. P.U. van Ryhn, Groningen.
Trustees of the Winchester Observatory. Records
Asteroid Observing Book – N.H. Field Plates
Roy, Arthur J. – Yale Index to Star Catalogues , list of omissions
Yale College Observatory, Indexes for Foreign and Domestic Observatory Libraries, Directors, etc.
Yale College Observatory, Thermometric Bureau Index, Book no. 60
Transit of Venus in 1874. Atlas to Part II.
Lunar Orbiter I, II, III Photographs
Atlas der Himmelszone zwischen 1° und 23° Sublicher Declination ; Schonenfield, p. 41-64
Lunar Orbiter I, II, III Photographs
Atlas des Nördlichen Gestrirnten Himmels - Bonn, 1855
Zonas de Exploración Cielo Austral , Cordoba
